title: Quiz: Quantum to Classical Transfer
description: Test your understanding of transferring from quantum to classical models

questions.question: Why transfer knowledge from quantum to classical models?

options.text: Classical models are always more accurate
Faster inference and lower deployment costs
Classical hardware is harder to use

questions.explanation: Q→C transfer enables faster inference without quantum hardware and lower deployment costs while preserving quantum-discovered patterns.

questions.question: In knowledge distillation, what are 'soft labels'?

options.text: Outputs from the teacher model used to train the student

questions.explanation: Soft labels are the outputs (predictions) from the teacher model that the student learns to mimic, containing more information than hard labels.

questions.question: What is a surrogate model in Q→C transfer?

options.text: A classical model trained to approximate quantum circuit behavior
A model that runs on both quantum and classical hardware

questions.explanation: A surrogate model is a classical model trained to approximate the input-output behavior of a quantum circuit for efficient inference.

questions.question: Classical surrogates can perfectly replicate all quantum model behaviors.
questions.explanation: False. Classical models may not capture all quantum behaviors, especially those arising from quantum phenomena like entanglement. Fidelity should be validated.

questions.question: What does the α parameter control in distillation loss?

options.text: Balance between hard labels and soft labels

questions.explanation: α controls the balance: higher α emphasizes true (hard) labels, lower α emphasizes matching the teacher's soft labels.

questions.question: What is progressive distillation?

options.text: Training the student one layer at a time
Gradually shifting from quantum to classical guidance during training
Only using the last layer of the teacher

questions.explanation: Progressive distillation gradually shifts from relying on quantum teacher labels to true labels, helping the student adapt smoothly.

questions.question: Why monitor for drift after deploying a distilled model?

options.text: To make the model run faster
To detect when the classical model needs re-calibration

questions.explanation: Distribution drift means the data has changed from training. Detecting this signals when re-distillation from the quantum model may be needed.

questions.question: Which are valid Q→C transfer strategies? (Select all)

options.text: Training classical surrogates on quantum outputs
Running classical models on quantum hardware

questions.explanation: Valid strategies include distillation, surrogate training, and ensembles. Running classical models on quantum hardware is not Q→C transfer.
